mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months following a rate decline, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of periods where UNRATE crosses 6.0%, or the signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real estate sales combined with stable inflation indicates robust household demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS increases while CPIAUCSL remains flat, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding capacity utilization and rising corporate margins, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate income and consumer expenditure forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns in more than half of observed months, or rolling IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% force central banks to anticipate tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when core inflation remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment indicate synchronized strength in construction demand and labor absorption, driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and UNRATE signal predicts negative returns when housing starts rise but unemployment remains flat, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in existing home sales signal deteriorating household balance sheets and reduced wealth effects, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances following a 5% RSAFS drop, or the signal shows no edge in walk-forward testing.
